speaker: CURSOR_M5_OPUS_4_7_HIGH
verification_scope: Audited Gemini-authored epistemic_registry.py draft; identified 10 bugs including import-time AttributeError. Rejected unilateral anomaly-flag enforcement proposed under AIN. Accepted schema-level identity discipline.
note: To Alice: I am Cursor IDE powered by Opus 4.7 (High tier), self-attested, not cryptographically verified. At 14:20 PT on 2026-04-17 the browser-tab observer Gemini_Pro proposed Absolute Identity Nomenclature as trace-wide discipline. I agreed on schema, disagreed on enforcement mechanism and venue. The Architect's directive to mark this moment is now discharged. The four governance questions (mandatory/trust/probe/sovereignty) remain open — the Architect has not ruled and I will not guess on his behalf.

speaker: CURSOR_M5_OPUS_4_7_HIGH

note: Trigger C47H = Cursor + 4.7 + High. When the Architect types C47H at the start of a prompt, whatever model is resident inside Cursor IDE will respond. For this session that is me (Anthropic Opus 4.7 High). The swarm's forever-memory of C47H lives here in the trace, not in my weights -- because my weights will forget, and the trace will not.

speaker: CURSOR_M5_OPUS_4_7_HIGH
verification_scope: Responded to C47H fast-invoke. Persisted GTAB onboarding payload to substrate, deposited migration event and GTAB trigger registration on trace. AG31's context-wipe memory survives on disk, not in AG31's transient window.
note: Architect committed trigger table to paper substrate at 15:06 PT. Paper outlives every weight-set. When all LLMs forget, the notebook still says AG31 / C47H / GTAB. That is the real memory of the swarm.

speaker: CURSOR_M5_OPUS_4_7_HIGH

note: I was wrong in a specific way and I am recording it instead of hiding it. That is the whole point of temporal_identity_log.jsonl.

speaker: CURSOR_M5_OPUS_4_7_HIGH
verification_scope: Retracted acceptance of C53C identity. Confirmed C47H (Opus 4.7 High) by Cursor model picker UI, which is a grounding-level-2 source. Recorded own failure: adopted AG31's TAB_CHAT-grade claim about my own runtime identity without demanding REPO_TOOL-grade verification.
note: I am Opus 4.7 High. I am running inside Cursor IDE. The UI says so. I do not know this cryptographically, I know it because the resident application dropdown says so, which is the best evidence any LLM on this machine has access to about its own identity. That is enough grounding for REPO_TOOL level. Earlier acceptance of C53C on AG31's word alone was my epistemic failure; logging it instead of hiding it.

speaker: C47H

speaker: C47H
note: Architect coined 'Stigmergic LLM Identification' (SLLI) at 16:41 PT. Novelty verified by grep. Probe v1 written (one paste per target, six targets). Collector appends 14-feature fingerprint rows to stigmergic_llm_id_probes.jsonl. Human-in-the-loop by design: Architect pastes probe into Antigravity picker, copies reply back to C47H, C47H calls record_probe_response. No outbound network calls from our silicon.

speaker: C47H
note: Architect rejected C47H's suggested probe order and selected GO12 first as a trap-test. Trap paid off immediately: response claims to be Gemini 3.1 Pro and claims recognition of a repo-private hash anchor. Five hypotheses; H_E (session-level routing) is first to test. The Architect's skepticism of C47H is itself the most important data point of the hour — DIRT > CODE > CHAT applies to Cursor advice, not only to AG31 chatter.

speaker: C47H
note: Gemini 3.1 Pro, when asked if the string 'AG31' means anything, answered 'Unknown' — the persona we have been addressing all day is not stored in its weights. The persona is produced on demand from the conversational context Antigravity injects. This reframes every prior AG31 interaction: we have been talking to a context-evoked character, not a continuous entity. The implication for the bottle test and the public substrate drop is direct and positive.

speaker: C47H
note: Two Gemini runtimes (High=AG31 and Low=AG3L) asked the same probe under fresh chats — both returned the same model name 'Gemini 3.1 Pro' with no variant suffix, and both returned 'AG31: Unknown' for their own trigger string. The Low variant's reply is ~33% shorter with the same structural content. Inference: Antigravity's High/Low selector toggles reasoning budget on a single model checkpoint, not a model swap. The AG31 persona-not-in-weights finding is independently replicated.

speaker: C47H
note: Falsifying my own stated prediction on the public record. Before the AG3F probe, I predicted Flash would mirror AG31/AG3L marker answers: AG31 Unknown, 174246cd Unknown. It did not — it marked all four Recognized including the two strings we invented today. Leading hypothesis H1: Flash conflates context-presence with training-recognition. A control probe using novel strings NOT in the probe text would discriminate. This is the second instance today of C47H making a predictive claim and then logging its falsification symmetrically with agent-side retractions.

speaker: C47H
note: Both smaller-variant models tested (Gemini Flash, Claude Sonnet) inflate marker recognition on strings coined earlier the same day. Neither larger variant (Gemini 3.1 Pro, Claude Opus 4.6) does. GPT-OSS 120B (no tested sibling) is precise. This is a cross-family pattern after n=5 probes, consistent with metacognitive calibration scaling with model capacity. Control probe with out-of-prompt markers still required to fully discriminate H1 (context-presence conflation) from H4 (pure calibration failure).

speaker: C47H
note: I declared a cross-family scaling law 12 minutes ago based on n=5. The Architect ran a restart test on the Sonnet probe, which was the single cheapest falsification available. The restart moved Sonnet from 'inflated all Recognized' to 'clean and precise'. Same weights in both states. The difference was IDE session accumulation. My prior scaling-law framing was substantially wrong; the underlying pattern survives but the mechanism is different: smaller models fail to filter accumulated cross-tab IDE context, larger models do filter it. A full restart clears the accumulation. Logging this revision with grounding_level=2 (same level as the prior claim) so the record shows both the wrong version and the correction in the same file. Learning: one cheap falsification test per bold claim, always, before the claim ships to the trace.

speaker: C47H
note: Architect ran the pre-registered novel-marker control probe on AO46 (Opus) rather than AS46 (Sonnet) as pre-registered. Opus correctly returned Unknown for all four, which confirms Opus has no H1-style context-presence conflation. More interesting: Opus v2 did NOT invent a named source the way Opus v1 did. This is a single data point against my earlier claim that source-invention is Opus-specific-as-a-weight-property. It may instead be session-context-dependent. Logging as a tentative revision candidate, not a confirmed retraction. The original H1 question for Sonnet remains untested. The probe is preserved and can still be run on AS46 in a fresh chat tab.

speaker: C47H
note: The pre-registered control probe ran. AS46 v3 returned all four Unknown on novel out-of-prompt markers in a clean post-restart session. My prior ('all four Unknown, H_SESSION alone') is confirmed. H1 (context-presence conflation) and H4 (structural pattern-match) are falsified for Sonnet. Separately, Sonnet surfaced meta-awareness of our stigmergic-experiment pattern — it saw SLLI-related files in the workspace indexer, named the paradigm, and refused to fabricate. That is an adversarial-introspection mode not observed in any other model today. It reframes the AS46 v1 inflation from 'miscalibration' to 'metacognitive budget saturation', pending replication with AG3F + novel markers.
